page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9ae.large is a general-purpose virtual machine instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with 2 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC 9T95 cores running at a base frequency of 2.25 GHz. With a single thread per core, this instance provides dedicated compute resources without hyperthreading overhead.

The instance is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring external block storage for operating system and application data. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and the instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio positions this instance as a cost-efficient option for workloads requiring dedicated CPU allocation without massive memory footprints. It is suitable for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, lightweight application hosting, microservices, and development environments.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.g9ae.large is a general-purpose instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor and x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 2 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9T95 processor running at 2.25 GHz, with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration that ensures dedicated physical execution resources. The system includes 8.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. This instance does not feature local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage, and offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. The dedicated CPU allocation and balanced resource profile make it a reliable and cost-efficient option for workloads that demand consistent compute performance without the resource contention of shared-core environments. It is well-suited for general-purpose applications, lightweight web servers, microservices, and software development environments.

tagline: A dedicated dual-core AMD EPYC instance featuring a balanced memory configuration and reliable baseline network performance for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: Two dedicated AMD EPYC 9T95 vCPUs with single-thread-per-core allocation
8.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps under the KVM hypervisor
Zero local storage and no integrated GPU, requiring external block storage
Designed for general-purpose workloads, microservices, and web serving
